tasks.title: Write config write commands spec
tasks.body:
Create a spec document via 'wolfcastle spec create' that defines all four config write commands: set, unset, append, remove.

The spec must cover:

1. CLI interface for each command:
   - 'wolfcastle config set <key> <value> [--tier local|custom]' — sets a config key using dot-notation. Value is parsed as JSON (bare strings allowed). Tier defaults to local.
   - 'wolfcastle config unset <key> [--tier local|custom]' — removes a key by writing null (deletion semantics via DeepMerge). Tier defaults to local.
   - 'wolfcastle config append <key> <value> [--tier local|custom]' — appends a value to an existing array. Tier defaults to local.
   - 'wolfcastle config remove <key> <value> [--tier local|custom]' — removes a value from an array. Tier defaults to local.

2. Dot-notation path semantics:
   - Paths like 'daemon.poll_interval_seconds' or 'pipeline.stages.audit.enabled' split on dots
   - No array indexing (e.g., 'commands[0]' is NOT supported)
   - Map keys within nested maps (like pipeline.stages, models) addressed by their key name
   - Intermediate paths that don't exist are created automatically for set

3. Value parsing:
   - Values are first tried as JSON (numbers, booleans, null, objects, arrays)
   - If JSON parse fails, the raw string is used as a JSON string value
   - This means 'wolfcastle config set logs.max_files 5' sets an integer, and 'wolfcastle config set identity.user alice' sets a string

4. Tier targeting:
   - --tier flag accepts 'local' or 'custom' only (writing to base is not allowed)
   - Default tier is 'local'
   - Note existing ADR: WithConfig writes to custom tier, but user-facing CLI defaults to local per scope definition

5. Read-modify-write flow:
   - Read the current tier overlay file (may be empty/missing)
   - Apply the mutation to the overlay map
   - Write the modified overlay back to the tier file
   - Load the fully merged config (all tiers + defaults) and run ValidateStructure
   - If validation fails, roll back the tier file and return the validation error
   - If validation passes, print a success message

6. Output:
   - Human mode: print 'Set <key> = <value> in <tier>/config.json' (or equivalent for other commands)
   - JSON mode: output.Ok envelope with the key, value, and tier

7. Error conditions:
   - Invalid tier name
   - Malformed dot-notation key (empty segments, trailing dots)
   - config append/remove on a non-array value
   - config remove for a value not present in the array
   - Validation failure after write (must roll back)
   - Cannot write to base tier

Reference existing patterns: cmd/config/show.go for CLI structure, readTierFile for tier reading, internal/config/repository.go for WriteCustom/WriteLocal, internal/config/merge.go for DeepMerge semantics.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Config Write Spec is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Wrote config write commands spec covering set/unset/append/remove with full CLI interface, dot-notation semantics, value parsing, tier targeting, read-modify-write with rollback, output formats, and error conditions

Audit reviewed 1 file (178 lines): .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-21T14-23Z-config-write-commands.md. PASS. Findings: (1) Duplicate H1 heading on lines 1 and 3, fixed directly and committed. (2) Spec accurately references existing code: readTierFile in cmd/config/show.go, WriteCustom/WriteLocal in internal/config/repository.go, DeepMerge null-deletion semantics in internal/config/merge.go, ValidateStructure in internal/config/validate.go. (3) The unset-via-nil claim is correct: Go json.Marshal serializes nil map values as JSON null, and DeepMerge deletes keys with nil values. (4) AAR action item about extracting readTierFile to a shared helper is reasonable implementation guidance, not an audit concern. Quality: thorough, well-structured spec with clear error tables, correct tier semantics, and sound transactional rollback design. Build and tests pass.

audit.result_summary: Produced a spec defining four config write commands (set, unset, append, remove) with full CLI interface, dot-notation path semantics, JSON value parsing, tier targeting, transactional read-modify-write with validation rollback, human and JSON output formats, and comprehensive error conditions. All code references verified against the codebase. Audit found and fixed one duplicate heading; no substantive issues.

audit.objective: Verify the config write commands spec is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Read the full spec (178 lines). Verified all code references against the actual codebase: readTierFile, WriteCustom/WriteLocal, DeepMerge null-deletion, ValidateStructure. Confirmed the nil-to-JSON-null serialization chain. Found and fixed a duplicate H1 heading. Ran build and full test suite, both clean.
went_well: Spec is well-structured with clear sections, accurate code references, and a complete error condition table
The read-modify-write flow with rollback is well-designed and matches existing ConfigRepository contracts
improvements: None significant; the duplicate heading was the only issue
action_items: Implementation tasks should verify readTierFile extraction as noted in the AAR from task-0001

task-0001.objective: Write a spec defining the four config write commands (set, unset, append, remove)
task-0001.what_happened: Studied the existing config show spec, ConfigRepository contract, DeepMerge semantics, and the WithConfig ADR. Wrote a spec covering CLI interface, dot-notation path semantics, value parsing, tier targeting, read-modify-write with validation rollback, output formats, and error conditions.
went_well: The existing config show spec provided a clear template for structure and level of detail
DeepMerge null-deletion semantics fit naturally into the unset command design
improvements: The spec references readTierFile in cmd/config/show.go which may need extraction to a shared location during implementation
action_items: Implementation tasks should extract readTierFile and the dot-notation path walker into shared helpers within cmd/config/
